---
title: "Assignment 1"
---

## Data Analysis

In this section, the `polls_us_election_2016` dataset from the **dslabs** package is analyzed using R.

```{r}
library(dslabs)
library(dplyr)

# Load dataset
data("polls_us_election_2016")
data <- polls_us_election_2016

# Personal variables
my_first <- "Bora"
my_birth_year <- 2004

# Calculate k
k <- (nchar(my_first) + my_birth_year) %% 15 + 8
k

# Display rows from original dataset
if (k %% 2 == 0) {
  head(data, k)
} else {
  tail(data, k)
}

# Total NA values
sum(is.na(data))

# NA values per column (top 8)
na_per_column <- sort(colSums(is.na(data)), decreasing = TRUE)
head(na_per_column, 8)

# Replace NA values
new_data <- data %>%
  mutate(
    across(where(is.numeric), ~ ifelse(is.na(.), my_birth_year + k, .)),
    across(where(is.character), ~ ifelse(is.na(.), paste0(my_first, "_", k), .)),
    across(where(is.factor), ~ {
      x <- as.character(.)
      x[is.na(x)] <- paste0(my_first, "_", k)
      x
    })
  )

# Display rows from new dataset
if (k %% 2 == 0) {
  head(new_data, k)
} else {
  tail(new_data, k)
}

# Remaining NA values
sum(is.na(new_data))

# anyNA check
anyNA(new_data)
```
